title: Pre-Qualification Requirements for Manning Agency License
content:
Any person applying for a license to operate a manning agency shall file a written application with the Administration, together with the following requirements:

A. Business Name Certificate issued by the Department of Trade and Industry (DTI) for single proprietorship; or certified copy of Articles of Partnership or Articles of Incorporation registered with SEC for partnership or corporation.

B. Proof of financial capacity:
  i. Bank certificate showing deposit of at least PhP5,000,000.00 with authority to examine the bank account;
  ii. Duly filed Income Tax Returns for last two years of proprietor, partners, board members, major stockholders and official receipts showing payment of income tax;
  iii. Proof of sources of investment.

C. Proof of existence of new principal:
  i. POLO verified and/or consulate authenticated Manning Agreement between manning agency and new principal;
  ii. Principal's profile including valid business license or commercial registration with English translation, business activities, years in operation, number of seafarers.

D. Proof of possession by sole proprietor, managing partner, president or CEO of bachelor's degree or at least four years experience in shipping-related or human resource management or experience in heading/managing shipping or manpower business.

E. Valid clearances from NBI and Anti-Illegal Recruitment Branch for proprietor, partners, board members. For foreign board members, authenticated police clearance from country of origin and affidavit declaring no criminal conviction or pending case for illegal recruitment or crime involving moral turpitude.

F. Duly notarized undertaking by sole proprietor, managing partner, or president stating commitments including selection of medically fit recruits, assuming liabilities, guaranteeing compliance with labor laws and international regulations, ethical standards, repatriation responsibilities, deployment targets, and others as detailed.

G. For corporation or partnership, notarized undertaking by officers/directors or partners of joint and several liability for claims/damages to seafarers.

H. List of officials and personnel involved in recruitment with appointments, bio-data, passport-size pictures, NBI clearances, and affidavits of no conviction or pending criminal case for illegal recruitment or moral turpitude.

I. Certificate of attendance in Pre-Licensing Orientation Seminar (PLOS) by key officers.

J. Flowchart detailing recruitment procedures, documentary requirements, briefings, authorized fees, deployment timeframes, responsible officers, and process cycle times.

K. Four-year business plan detailing financial, market, operational viability, projected income, and risk management plan.
